_meta.purpose: Bidirectional map between this project's G1-G7 / S1-S6 taxonomy and SlideAudit (UIST'25, arXiv 2508.03630) 19-dim design-deficiency taxonomy. All Part 3 quantitative eval/summary output is reported in SlideAudit-canonical categories for direct comparability; off-taxonomy items go to the OTHER bucket (A.3.1).
_meta.g7_status: our-extension (SlideAudit has no linter-blind render-containment class); refines 'Content Overflow/Cut-off' with the structural criterion declared-bbox-legal => geometry-linter-blind

G6_MARGIN_VIOLATION.note: SlideAudit's space-distribution dim subsumes our safe-margin bleed.

G7_RENDER_CONTAINMENT_OVERFLOW.note: OUR EXTENSION. Same visual phenomenon as Content Overflow/Cut-off but adds the structural criterion declared-bbox-legal => linter-blind; the image-objectfit variant also touches Improper Image Sizing.

S1_TITLE_BODY_MISMATCH.note: Content-semantic (title vs body topic). SlideAudit's per-slide design taxonomy has no content-correctness dim.

S2_NARRATIVE_ORDER_BREAK.note: Deck-level narrative ordering. SlideAudit annotates single slides only.

S3_TERMINOLOGY_INCONSISTENCY.note: Cross-slide terminology. Routed to a symbolic term-consistency linter in this project; SlideAudit has no analog.

S5_MISSING_LOGIC_SECTION.note: Deck-level completeness. SlideAudit annotates single slides only.

S6_IMAGE_TEXT_CONTRADICTION.note: Image-text factual contradiction is content-semantic; SlideAudit's nearest dim ('Irrelevant Visual Content') is about relevance, not contradiction.

Cluttered Layout.note: Density-adjacent but holistic; not a clean 1:1.

Poor Text Hierarchy.note: Hierarchy of headings vs body; loose.

Improper Image Sizing.note: Image rendered too large/small for its frame; our G7 image-objectfit variant is a structural special case.

other_bucket.definition: Off-taxonomy free-form findings (C1 Stage-2) that map to no defined G/S type go here with their original text preserved. Cross-sample recurrence in OTHER => candidate new defect class (Phase 5). OTHER is excluded from defined-class precision/recall but its magnitude and recurrence are reported separately.
